mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales over the prior three months signal strengthening household demand and near-term earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward observations or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the federal funds rate signals tighter discount-rate conditions and lower equity valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average after being below it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS upward crossings produce non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns or if negative hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession-risk repricing, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the federal funds rate also rises, inflation pressure and policy tightening jointly raise discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates versus its prior 3-month growth and FEDFUNDS increases over the same 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports CPIAUCSL=228.527 and FEDFUNDS=0.16
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase trigger has a non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 forward return or if its downside hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 forward returns than negative 6-month INDPRO growth at p <= 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening cyclical demand and reduced confidence-sensitive activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST falling more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate identify broad labor-market strengthening, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports PAYEMS=133009.0 and UNRATE=8.2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger produces an average 5-day S&P 500 forward return that is not greater than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and consumer prices are still rising year over year, real policy accommodation supports risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 and CPIAUCSL is higher than its level 12 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports FEDFUNDS=0.16 and CPIAUCSL=228.527
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 1.0 with positive year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of observations.
